rules: clients are not the control plane
workers are adapters behind a governed contract
terminal is a governed tool surface, not the primary UX
raw model invoke and managed worker execution stay separate

recommended_milestones.name: session_task_worker_contract
recommended_milestones.goal: define the stable control-plane contract that all future clients and workers must use
recommended_milestones.exit_gate: one stable control-plane contract exists that chat and IDE clients can use without special-case behavior

recommended_milestones.name: operator_chat_surface
recommended_milestones.goal: build the operator-first chat surface on top of the shared control-plane contract
recommended_milestones.exit_gate: non-technical operators can run governed sessions without using terminal or IDE surfaces

recommended_milestones.name: worker_adapter_pack
recommended_milestones.goal: add managed worker execution behind the control-plane contract
recommended_milestones.exit_gate: at least one managed worker can execute tasks through the session contract with approval, audit, and evidence capture

recommended_milestones.name: ide_and_enterprise_ingress_pack
recommended_milestones.goal: extend the same governed system to developer and enterprise ingress surfaces
recommended_milestones.exit_gate: developers and non-developers can enter the same governed system through different surfaces with a shared audit and evidence model
current_parallel_progress: first VS Code client slice landed in clients/vscode-agentops
native thread list reads v1alpha2 runtime tasks
native thread review reads sessions, timeline, and events stream
managed worker review renders approvals, tool actions, evidence, and worker events from native session state
IDE-native approval and tool-proposal decisions are wired on the same native session contract
governed turn submission is wired from the IDE surface through the v1alpha1 integration invoke path using the current taskId
first CLI ingress slice landed in clients/cli-agentops
CLI thread review composes native task, session, timeline, approval, tool_action, evidence, and event-stream state without introducing another orchestration path
CLI approval decisions, tool-proposal decisions, and governed turn submission are wired on the same native session contract used by desktop chat and VS Code
first ticket workflow ingress slice landed in clients/workflow-agentops
workflow intake creates native governed tasks with reusable workflow annotations and optional first governed turns on the same session contract
workflow status renders native task, session, timeline, proposal, and recent-event state as text, json, or ticket-comment output without introducing a separate orchestration path
first chatops ingress slice landed in clients/chatops-agentops
chatops intake creates native governed tasks from external conversation context and optionally starts the first governed turn on the same session contract
chatops status renders native task, session, timeline, proposal, and recent-event state as text, json, or thread-ready update output without introducing a separate orchestration path
recommended_milestones.next_step: deepen chatops on the same reusable M16 or M18 contract by adding native approval and proposal decision actions while keeping VS Code, CLI, workflow, and chatops ingress on the same native control-plane model

recommended_milestones.name: enterprise_hardening_and_rollout_pack
recommended_milestones.goal: close the enterprise deployment posture gaps after client and worker surfaces exist
recommended_milestones.exit_gate: Fortune 500 deployment posture is supported across UI, IDE, API, and worker surfaces with clear governance boundaries

recommended_immediate_next_step: continue M19 from the validated VS Code, CLI, workflow, and chatops slices by adding native approval and proposal decision actions to chatops on the same native session contract without forking the control-plane model
parallel_execution_note: M16 architecture/spec work may begin while M15 remains blocked on Windows execution-host proof, but M15 must not be marked complete until Phase D and final launch sign-off are closed.
